item.title: 🇮🇷 IRGC seizes wartime power as Irans Supreme Leader role diminishes
item.description: Two months into war with the US and Israel, Irans Revolutionary Guards have consolidated decision-making power, with Mojtaba Khamenei reduced to legitimizing generals decisions rather than issuing directives. The shift may harden Tehrans stance in any renewed talks with Washington.

item.title: 🇯🇵🇨🇳 Chinese research vessel departs near Japans southwest islands after two weeks
item.description: A Chinese research vessel departed waters near Japans Nansei Islands after a two-week presence, marking the third such incident in a month. Japans Defense Ministry is monitoring increased Chinese maritime activity near its southwestern islands.

item.title: 🇰🇷⚔️ KAI and Bell partner on South Koreas next-generation utility helicopter
item.description: Korea Aerospace Industries and Bell Textron signed a memorandum of understanding to develop South Koreas next-generation high-speed medium utility helicopter. The partnership advances Seouls military modernization amid regional tensions.

item.title: 🇸🇩👶 UN warns five million children in Darfur face extreme deprivation as Sudan war enters fourth year
item.description: UNICEF issued an emergency warning over the humanitarian crisis in Sudans Darfur region, where five million children face extreme hunger, violence and displacement as the civil war continues into its fourth year.

item.title: 🇪🇺📱 EU charges Meta for failing to keep children under 13 off Facebook and Instagram
item.description: The European Commission issued preliminary findings that Meta breached EU digital rules by inadequately preventing children under 13 from accessing Facebook and Instagram, potentially exposing them to age-inappropriate content.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Hegseth faces Congress for first time since Iran war began
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth will face questioning from lawmakers Wednesday for the first time since the Iran war began, addressing military budgets and strategy amid ongoing Middle East conflict.

item.title: 🇰🇷 South Korean court sentences former President Yoon to seven years in prison
item.description: A South Korean appeals court sentenced ousted President Yoon Suk Yeol to seven years in prison for resisting arrest during investigations into his martial law declaration, increasing his previous sentence.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Taiwan tops Beijings agenda ahead of Trump-Xi summit
item.description: Taiwan will be a central topic when Chinese President Xi Jinping meets US President Donald Trump, with Beijing seeking clarity on Washingtons commitment to the One China policy amid escalating tensions.

item.title: 🇬🇧 How the Iran war is hitting the UK
item.description: Britains economy and energy security face mounting pressure from the ongoing Iran conflict, with implications for fuel prices, supply chains and diplomatic relations with Gulf states.

item.title: 🇪🇺 Meta faces EU charges over Instagram and Facebook age verification
item.description: European regulators charged Meta with breaching digital services rules by failing to adequately prevent children under 13 from accessing Instagram and Facebook, exposing them to age-inappropriate content.

item.title: 🛢️ OPEC and OPEC+ explained as UAE announces exit
item.description: The United Arab Emirates announced its departure from OPEC effective May 1, ending nearly six decades of membership and dealing a significant blow to the oil cartels influence over global markets.

item.title: 🇬🇧 King Charles says Iran should not have nuclear weapons in Congress speech
item.description: King Charles III addressed US Congress during his state visit, emphasizing the US-UK alliance and noting that Britain does not want Iran to possess nuclear weapons, amid ongoing conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: 🇵🇱 Belarus releases Polish-Belarusian journalist in prisoner exchange
item.description: Belarus released Andrzej Poczobut, a prominent Polish-Belarusian journalist and activist, as part of a prisoner swap at the border with Poland. A Russian archaeologist was freed by Warsaw in return.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Putin says Ukraine has intensified attacks on civilian targets in Russia
item.description: President Vladimir Putin said Ukraine has stepped up attacks on civilian infrastructure in Russia, citing a recent drone strike on an oil refinery in the southern city of Tuapse.

item.title: 🇲🇱 Mali military leader vows to neutralize attackers after coordinated assault
item.description: Malis military leader Assimi Goita made his first public appearance since insurgents launched coordinated attacks over the weekend, vowing to neutralize those responsible as security concerns mount.

item.title: 🌍 Europe named fastest-warming continent in climate report
item.description: Almost all of Europe experienced above-average heat in 2025, a year that broke records for wildfires, sea temperatures and heatwaves as climate change worsens, EU scientists and the World Meteorological Organization reported.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China suspends autonomous driving permits after Baidu incident
item.description: Chinese regulators suspended new permits for autonomous driving after a Baidu vehicle incident, raising questions about the pace of self-driving technology deployment in China.

item.title: 🇲🇽 Mexican prosecutors to probe unauthorized CIA presence after fatal crash
item.description: Mexicos Specialized Regional Control Prosecutors Office will open case files investigating the unauthorized presence of CIA agents in the country following a fatal vehicle crash.

item.title: 🇦🇺 Australian state launches first gas tender in years amid energy security push
item.description: New South Wales launched its first natural gas exploration tender in a decade as Australia works to secure domestic energy supplies amid global market volatility from the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia to mark Victory Day parade without military equipment for first time since Ukraine invasion
item.description: Russias traditional Victory Day parade marking the defeat of Nazi Germany will take place without military equipment for the first time since the 2022 invasion of Ukraine, reflecting resource constraints and ongoing combat operations.

item.title: 💰 World Bank forecasts 24% surge in energy prices in 2026 due to Middle East war
item.description: The World Bank projected energy prices will surge 24% in 2026 due to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, highlighting the global economic impact of the Iran war on commodities.

item.title: 🇨🇳 Taiwan minister visits disputed South China Sea islet amid tensions
item.description: Taiwans foreign minister said she visited a second islet in the disputed South China Sea, asserting territorial claims amid competing assertions from China and other regional powers.

item.title: 🇵🇭 Philippines not concerned Iran war will distract US from region, defense chief says
item.description: The Philippines defense secretary said Manila is not worried about any reduction in US deterrence capabilities in the Indo-Pacific due to the Middle East war, though warned China could try to seize any perceived opening.

item.title: 🇸🇩 Children at breaking point in Darfur amid extreme hunger and violence, UN says
item.description: Five million children across Sudans Darfur region face extreme deprivation, the UN childrens agency warned, issuing an emergency alert as the civil war enters its fourth year.
